content.problemStatement:
Given a grid of dimensions n x n. A rat is placed at coordinates (0, 0) and wants to reach at coordinates (n-1, n-1). Find all possible paths that rat can take to travel from (0, 0) to (n-1, n-1). The directions in which rat can move are 'U' (up) , 'D' (down) , 'L' (left) , 'R' (right). 
The value 0 in grid denotes that the cell is blocked and rat cannot use that cell for travelling, whereas value 1 represents that rat can travel through the cell. If the cell (0, 0) has 0 value, then mouse cannot move to any other cell.
constraints: The maze is a square grid of size n x n
Each cell in the maze is either 0 (blocked) or 1 (open)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, representing the size of the maze. The next n lines contain n space-separated integers, representing the maze.
content.outputFormat: Print all possible paths from the top-left cell to the bottom-right cell, with each path represented as a string of 'U', 'D', 'L', and 'R' characters.

hidden.label: Tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use a recursive backtracking approach to explore all possible paths from the top-left cell to the bottom-right cell.
